item.ptxt: There are three types of OBD system depending on the vehicle specification. For identification, refer to OBD System Description .
For type A, in case that MIL turns on, the malfunction(s) is detected in either ECM or TCM and it stores the trouble information (DTC, freeze flame data, etc.) in its memory.
According to the varieties of scan tool, check the trouble information as follows.
It is necessary to check both ECM and TCM separately because the trouble information of both modules cannot be read and displayed at once.
CAN communication OBD-II generic scan tool:
It is not necessary to check both ECM and TCM separately because the trouble information of both modules can be read and displayed at once.

s1.ptxt: Connect SUZUKI scan tool to DLC (1).
Read DTC, pending DTC and freeze frame data according to instructions displayed on scan tool and print it down. Refer to scan tool operator’s manual for further details.
If communication between scan tool and TCM is not possible, go to CAN Communication Check .
After completing check, set ignition OFF and disconnect SUZUKI scan tool from DLC.
